title: Contributing to envar
text: outlines propose change envar. detailed discussion contributing tidyverse packages, please see development contributing guide code review principles.

title: Contributing to envar
text: can fix typos, spelling mistakes, grammatical errors documentation directly using GitHub web interface, long changes made source file. generally means ’ll need edit roxygen2 comments .R, .Rd file. can find .R file generates .Rd reading comment first line.

title: Contributing to envar
text: want make bigger change, ’s good idea first file issue make sure someone team agrees ’s needed. ’ve found bug, please file issue illustrates bug minimal reprex (also help write unit test, needed). See guide create great issue advice.

title: Contributing to envar
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("Andreacerofolini/envar", fork = TRUE). Install development dependencies devtools::install_dev_deps(), make sure package passes R CMD check running devtools::check(). R CMD check doesn’t pass cleanly, ’s good idea ask help continuing. Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). Make changes, commit git, create PR running usethis::pr_push(), following prompts browser. title PR briefly describe change. body PR contain Fixes #issue-number. user-facing changes, add bullet top NEWS.md (.e. just first header). Follow style described https://style.tidyverse.org/news.html.

title: Contributing to envar
text: New code follow tidyverse style guide. can use styler package apply styles, please don’t restyle code nothing PR. use roxygen2, Markdown syntax, documentation. use testthat unit tests. Contributions test cases included easier accept.

title: Contributing to envar
text: Please note envar project released Contributor Code Conduct. contributing project agree abide terms.

title: Download environmental variables in R
text: envar R package streamlines retrieval processing environmental variables ecological modelling. Selecting proper set environmental variables cornerstone ecological study often relegated simplistic use widespread variables. package facilitates careful selection heterogeneous set proximal drivers, allowing users integrate data various sources R workflows easily.

title: Download environmental variables in R
text: latest development version can installed gain access new functionality yet present latest official version. install latest development version GitHub, use following R code.

title: Download environmental variables in R
text: Please cite envar R package using publications, citation(s) associated source retrieved. citations specific source printed console download process. cite package, please use: Simoncini , Bertoncini M, Cerofolini , Dalpasso , Falaschi M, Lo Parrino E (2025) envar: R package streamline retrieval processing environmental variables.

title: Download environmental variables in R
text: provide short example showing envar R package can used retrieve process environmental variables specific use case. begin , load required packages. get set variables already cropped desired area study, presented rast file multiple layers corresponding different variables. instance, can visualize first layer produced (tree cover) follows.
code: # load packages library(envar)  # download variables (e.g., the percentage cover of trees and ice here) over a study area processed_vars = var_get(country="Italy", res = 1, crs="EPSG:3035") %>%    esalandcover(vars = c("trees", "ice")) # visualize a variable to have a confirmation that everything worked plot(processed_vars[[1]])

Download and process Global Accessibility Indicators — accessibility
text: x `SpatRaster`, `SpatVector`, `sf` object defining area locations extraction. vars Character vector variables, supplied canonical codes friendly names. ... Reserved future use.

text: - `x` raster: `SpatRaster` stack processed accessibility layers. - `x` contains points: `data.frame` extracted values.

title: Download and process Global Accessibility Indicators — accessibility
text: function allows users input either: - **canonical variable codes**, e.g. `"cities1"`, `"ports1"` - **human-readable names**, e.g. `"large cities"`, `"travel time cities 1"`,   `"small ports"`, `"ports 4"`, etc. automatically: - downloads selected files, - crops/resamples/masks match user-provided `SpatRaster`, **** - extracts values `x` point data. ## Citation use data, please cite: **Nelson, ., Weiss, D.J., van Etten, J. et al. (2019).** *suite global accessibility indicators.* Sci Data **6**, 266. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41597-019-0265-5 ## City Classifications | Code | Population minimum (>=) | Population maximum (<) | |——|————————-|————————| | 1    | 5,000,000               | 50,000,000             | | 2    | 1,000,000               | 5,000,000              | | 3    | 500,000                 | 1,000,000              | | 4    | 200,000                 | 500,000                | | 5    | 100,000                 | 200,000                | | 6    | 50,000                  | 100,000                | | 7    | 20,000                  | 50,000                 | | 8    | 10,000                  | 20,000                 | | 9    | 5,000                   | 10,000                 | | 10   | 20,000                  | 110,000,000            | | 11   | 50,000                  | 50,000,000             | | 12   | 5,000                   | 110,000,000            | ## Port Classifications | Code | Port size   | Number ports | |——|————-|—————–| | 1    | Large       | 160             | | 2    | Medium      | 361             | | 3    | Small       | 990             | | 4    | small  | 2,153           | | 5    |         | 3,778           | ## Available variables | Human-readable name examples     | Canonical variable code | |———————————-|————————-| | cities 1, huge cities            | cities1                 | | cities 2, large cities           | cities2                 | | cities 3, medium cities          | cities3                 | | cities 4                         | cities4                 | | cities 5                         | cities5                 | | cities 6                         | cities6                 | | cities 7                         | cities7                 | | cities 8                         | cities8                 | | cities 9                         | cities9                 | | cities 10                        | cities10                | | cities 11                        | cities11                | | cities 12                        | cities12                | | ports 1, large ports             | ports1                  | | ports 2, medium ports            | ports2                  | | ports 3, small ports             | ports3                  | | ports 4, small ports        | ports4                  | | ports 5, port                | ports5                  |

what: Check Variable Correlation and Multicollinearity — check
title: Check Variable Correlation and Multicollinearity — check
text: function analyzes collinearity among environmental variables using Pearson correlation Variance Inflation Factor (VIF). automatically handles data preparation (converting rasters data frames, removing NAs, dropping constant variables), generates correlation plot saved working directory, returns summary potential issues.

title: Check Variable Correlation and Multicollinearity — check
Check Variable Correlation and Multicollinearity — check
text: x SpatRaster (terra package) data.frame containing environmental variables analyzed.

title: Check Variable Correlation and Multicollinearity — check
text: list containing four elements: stack: original input object x. correlation_table: matrix Pearson correlation coefficients. vif_table: data frame containing VIF values, sorted descending order. correlation.summary: character vector summarizing warnings variables   correlation > 0.6 VIF > 3.

title: Check Variable Correlation and Multicollinearity — check
text: function performs following steps: Converts SpatRaster inputs data frame. Removes non-numeric columns rows containing NAs. Identifies removes variables zero variance (constant values). Calculates Pearson correlations saves plot "Corr_plot.png" current working directory. Calculates VIF using usdm::vif.

title: Check Variable Correlation and Multicollinearity — check
text: correlation plot saved high-resolution PNG file (Corr_plot.png) current working directory.

title: Check Variable Correlation and Multicollinearity — check
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Example with a data frame df <- data.frame(   temp = rnorm(100, mean = 20, sd = 5),   precip = rnorm(100, mean = 100, sd = 20),   elev = rnorm(100, mean = 500, sd = 100) )  # Add a correlated variable df$temp_correlated <- df$temp * 0.9 + rnorm(100)  result <- check(df)  # Inspect results print(result$vif_table) print(result$correlation.summary) } # }

title: Download CHELSA climate data — chelsa
text: function builds URLs downloads CHELSA climate data based variables, years, months, climate models, scenarios. Uses helper function `download_file` handle downloads. raster layer downloaded, processed (cropped/masked/resampled), added output stack immediately, temporary files deleted minimize memory use.

title: Download CHELSA climate data — chelsa
code: chelsa(   x,   vars,   years = NULL,   months = NULL,   gcm = NULL,   rcp = NULL,   ssp = NULL,   cruts_years = NULL,   ... )

title: Download CHELSA climate data — chelsa
text: x SpatRaster sf object define area interest vars character vector variables download (e.g., "tas", "pr", "bio") years character numeric vector years year ranges (e.g., "1981-2010", 2015) months numeric vector (1–12) specifying months download. NULL `years` single years, 12 months downloaded. gcm General Circulation Model(s) future projections rcp Representative Concentration Pathway CMIP5 data ssp Shared Socioeconomic Pathway CMIP6 data cruts_years Numeric vector. Years download CHELSAcruts (must 1901–2016) ... Additional arguments (currently unused)

title: Download CHELSA climate data — chelsa
text: SpatRaster stack data.frame extracted values

what: Download and process Köppen-Geiger climate classification maps — climatezones
title: Download and process Köppen-Geiger climate classification maps — climatezones
text: `climatezones()` downloads, processes, extracts variables **High-resolution (1 km) Köppen-Geiger maps** dataset. variable corresponds global GeoTIFF representing climate classification zones based historical data future CMIP6 projections.

title: Download and process Köppen-Geiger climate classification maps — climatezones
Download and process Köppen-Geiger climate classification maps — climatezones
text: x `SpatRaster`, `SpatVector`, `sf` object defining area locations extraction. vars Character vector. Defaults `"zones"`. Accepted aliases include: `"koppengeiger"`, `"climate"`, `"climatezones"`, `"koppen"`. years Character vector time periods. Defaults `"1991-2020"`. Accepts formats underscores hyphens (e.g., `"1901-1930"` `"1901_1930"`). ssp Numeric character vector Shared Socioeconomic Pathways. Required future projections (e.g., `126`, `585`). ... Reserved future use.

title: Download and process Köppen-Geiger climate classification maps — climatezones
text: - `x` raster: `SpatRaster` stack processed climate zone layers. - `x` contains points: `data.frame` extracted values.

title: Download and process Köppen-Geiger climate classification maps — climatezones
text: function automatically: - Checks dataset zip file (downloading necessary), - Extracts specific time periods SSP scenarios requested, - Crops/resamples/masks match user-provided `SpatRaster`, **** - Extracts values `x` point data. function specifically targets **0.01 degree resolution** layers (approximately 1km). ## Citation use data, please cite: **Beck, H.E., T.R. McVicar, N. Vergopolan, . Berg, N.J. Lutsko, . Dufour, Z. Zeng, X. Jiang, ..J.M. van Dijk, D.G. Miralles (2023).** *High-resolution (1 km) Köppen-Geiger maps 1901–2099 based constrained CMIP6 projections.* Scientific Data **10**, 724. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41597-023-02549-6 ## Available Arguments `vars` argument provided consistency defaults `"zones"`. Users control output primarily via `years` `ssp` arguments. ### Time Periods (`years`) **Historical:** * `"1901-1930"` * `"1931-1960"` * `"1961-1990"` * `"1991-2020"` **Future:** * `"2041-2070"` * `"2071-2099"` ### SSP Scenarios (`ssp`) Required future time period selected. * `119` (SSP1-1.9) * `126` (SSP1-2.6) * `245` (SSP2-4.5) * `370` (SSP3-7.0) * `434` (SSP4-3.4) * `460` (SSP4-6.0) * `585` (SSP5-8.5)

downloads selected files, - crops/resamples/masks match user-provided `SpatRaster`, **** - extracts values `x` point data. ## Citation use data, please cite: **Wilson , Jetz W (2016)** #' *Remotely Sensed High-Resolution Global Cloud Dynamics Predicting Ecosystem Biodiversity Distributions.* PLoS Biol 14(3): e1002415. https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pbio.1002415 ## Available variables | Human-readable name          | Canonical variable code              | |——————————|————————————–| | cloud forest prediction      | MODCF_CloudForestPrediction          | | inter-annual variability     | MODCF_interannualSD                  | | intra-annual variability     | MODCF_intraannualSD                  | | mean annual                  | MODCF_meanannual                     | | january mean (jan)           | MODCF_monthlymean_01                 | | february mean (feb)          | MODCF_monthlymean_02                 | | march mean (mar)             | MODCF_monthlymean_03                 | | april mean (apr)             | MODCF_monthlymean_04                 | | may mean                     | MODCF_monthlymean_05                 | | june mean (jun)              | MODCF_monthlymean_06                 | | july mean (jul)              | MODCF_monthlymean_07                 | | august mean (aug)            | MODCF_monthlymean_08                 | | september mean (sep)         | MODCF_monthlymean_09                 | | october mean (oct)           | MODCF_monthlymean_10                 | | november mean (nov)          | MODCF_monthlymean_11                 | | december mean (dec)          | MODCF_monthlymean_12                 | | seasonality concentration    | MODCF_seasonality_concentration      | | seasonality rgb              | MODCF_seasonality_rgb                | | seasonality theta            | MODCF_seasonality_theta              | | seasonality visual (visct)   | MODCF_seasonality_visct              | | spatial variability (1 deg)  | MODCF_spatialSD_1deg                 |

W. Jetz. (2014).** *global 1-km consensus land-cover product biodiversity ecosystem modeling.* Global Ecology Biogeography 23(9): 1031-1045. https://doi.org/10.1111/geb.12182 _Note: Users verify terms use EarthEnv data provided https://www.earthenv.org/._ ## Available variables | Human-readable name                 | Canonical variable code      | |————————————-|——————————| | Evergreen/Deciduous Needleleaf Trees| consensus_full_class_1       | | Evergreen Broadleaf Trees           | consensus_full_class_2       | | Deciduous Broadleaf Trees           | consensus_full_class_3       | | Mixed/Trees                   | consensus_full_class_4       | | Shrubs                              | consensus_full_class_5       | | Herbaceous Vegetation               | consensus_full_class_6       | | Cultivated Managed Vegetation   | consensus_full_class_7       | | Regularly Flooded Vegetation        | consensus_full_class_8       | | Urban/Built-                     | consensus_full_class_9       | | Snow/Ice                            | consensus_full_class_10      | | Barren                              | consensus_full_class_11      | | Open Water                          | consensus_full_class_12      |

text: function allows users input either: - **canonical variable codes**, e.g. `"wetland"`, `"tree"`, `"shannon"` - **human-readable names**, e.g. `"forest"`, `"agriculture"`,   `"surface water"`, `"simpson index"`, etc. automatically: - downloads selected files Figshare, - crops/resamples/masks match user-provided `SpatRaster`, **** - extracts values `x` point data. ## Citation use data, please cite: **Lo Parrino E, Simoncini , Ficetola GF, Falaschi M (2025)** #' *Global 1 km land cover macroecological modelling high resolution imagery.* #' Figshare. https://figshare.com/s/4e7dee46628b530aee03 ## Available variables | Human-readable name          | Canonical variable code              | |——————————|————————————–| | wetland / marsh              | wetland                              | | bare ground / soil           | bare                                 | | built / urban area           | built                                | | cropland / agriculture       | cropland                             | | grassland / meadow           | grass                                | | ice / snow / glacier         | ice                                  | | land percentage              | land_perc                            | | mangrove                     | mangrove                             | | moss / lichen                | moss                                 | | shrub / scrub                | shrub                                | | tree / forest                | tree                                 | | water / surface water        | water                                | | simpson index                | simpson                              | | shannon index                | shannon                              | | evenness index               | evenness                             |

title: Download and process Harmonized World Soil Database v2.0 — soil
text: function allows users input either: - **canonical variable names**, e.g. `"hwsd"` - **human-readable names**, e.g. `"soil"`, `"soil type"`, `"type"`, etc. automatically: - downloads source data (zipped), - unzips processes raster (`HWSD2.bil`), - crops/resamples/masks match user-provided `SpatRaster`, **** - extracts values `x` point data. ## Citation use HWSD v2.0 data, please cite: **FAO & IIASA. (2023).** *Harmonized World Soil Database v2.0.* Food Agriculture Organization United Nations, Rome International Institute Applied Systems Analysis, Laxenburg, Austria. https://www.fao.org/soils-portal/data-hub/soil-maps--databases/harmonized-world-soil-database-v20/en/ ## Available variables | Human-readable name          | Canonical variable code              | |——————————|————————————–| | soil                         | hwsd                                 | | type                         | hwsd                                 | | soil type                    | hwsd                                 | | soiltype                     | hwsd                                 | | hwsd                         | hwsd                                 |

title: Download and process SPECTRE environmental threat layers — spectre
text: `spectre()` downloads, processes, extracts variables **SPECTRE – Spatially Explicit ECosysTem ThREats** dataset. variable corresponds global Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF (COG) representing different anthropogenic climatic threat.

title: Download and process SPECTRE environmental threat layers — spectre
text: function allows users input either: - **canonical SPECTRE filenames**, e.g. `"1_1_MINING_AREA_cog"` - **human-readable names**, e.g. `"mining area"`, `"forest loss"`,   `"light night"`, `"aridity trend"`, etc. automatically: - downloads selected files, - crops/resamples/masks match user-provided `SpatRaster`, **** - extracts values `x` point data. ## Citation use SPECTRE data, please cite: **Branco, V. V., Capinha, C., Rocha, J., Correia, L. & Cardoso, P. (2024).** *SPECTRE: standardized global spatial data terrestrial SPecies ECosystems ThREats.* Global Ecology Biogeography, **34(1)**, e13949. https://doi.org/10.1111/geb.13949 _Note: Many SPECTRE variables derived external primary datasets. Users consult cite original sources listed SPECTRE supplementary materials._ ## Available variables | Human-readable name          | Canonical variable code              | |——————————|————————————–| | mining area                  | 1_1_MINING_AREA_cog                  | | hazard potential             | 1_2_HAZARD_POTENTIAL_cog             | | human density                | 1_3_HUMAN_DENSITY_cog                | | built area                   | 1_4_BUILT_AREA_cog                   | | road density                 | 1_5_ROAD_DENSITY_cog                 | | human footprint              | 1_6_FOOTPRINT_PERC_cog               | | impacted area                | 1_7_IMPACT_AREA_cog                  | | modified area                | 1_8_MODIF_AREA_cog                   | | human biomes                 | 1_9_HUMAN_BIOMES_cog                 | | fires                        | 1_10_FIRE_OCCUR_cog                  | | crops uni                    | 1_11_CROP_PERC_UNI_cog               | | crops iiasa                  | 1_12_CROP_PERC_IIASA_cog             | | livestock                    | 1_13_LIVESTOCK_MASS_cog              | | forest loss                  | 2_1_FOREST_LOSS_PERC_cog             | | forest trend                 | 2_2_FOREST_TREND_cog                 | | light night               | 3_1_LIGHT_MCDM2_cog                  | | temperature trends           | 5_1_TEMP_TRENDS_cog                  | | temperature significance     | 5_2_TEMP_SIGNIF_cog                  | | climate extremes             | 5_3_CLIM_EXTREME_cog                 | | climate velocity             | 5_4_CLIM_VELOCITY_cog                | | aridity trend                | 5_5_ARIDITY_TREND_cog                |

what: Download and process EarthEnv Topography layers — topography
title: Download and process EarthEnv Topography layers — topography
text: `topography()` downloads, processes, extracts variables **EarthEnv Topography** dataset. dataset provides global, cross-scale topographic variables suitable biodiversity ecosystem modeling.

title: Download and process EarthEnv Topography layers — topography
Download and process EarthEnv Topography layers — topography
text: x `SpatRaster`, `SpatVector`, `sf` object defining area locations extraction. vars Character vector variables, supplied canonical codes friendly names. algorithm Character. aggregation method/algorithm use. Common options: `"md"` (median), `"mn"` (mean), `"min"`, `"max"`, `"sd"`. Default `"md"`. topo_source Character. source data. Must `"GMTED"` (Global Multi-resolution Terrain Elevation Data) `"SRTM"` (Shuttle Radar Topography Mission). Default `"GMTED"`. ... Reserved future use.

title: Download and process EarthEnv Topography layers — topography
text: - `x` raster: `SpatRaster` stack processed topography layers. - `x` contains points: `data.frame` extracted values.

title: Download and process EarthEnv Topography layers — topography
text: function allows users input either: - **canonical EarthEnv variable names**, e.g. `"elevation"`, `"roughness"` - **human-readable names**, e.g. `"dem"`, `"slope"`, `"terrain ruggedness"`, etc. automatically: - downloads selected files based requested source algorithm, - crops/resamples/masks match user-provided `SpatRaster`, **** - extracts values `x` point data. ## Citation use EarthEnv Topography data, please cite: **Amatulli, G., Domisch, S., Tuanmu, M.-N., Parmentier, B., Ranipeta, .,** **Malczyk, J., Jetz, W. (2018).** *suite global, cross-scale topographic variables environmental biodiversity modeling.* Scientific Data 5: 180040. https://doi.org/10.1038/sdata.2018.40 ## Available variables | Human-readable name  | Canonical variable code | |———————-|————————-| | Elevation (DEM)      | elevation               | | Slope                | slope                   | | Aspect               | aspect                  | | Roughness            | roughness               | | TRI                  | tri                     | | TPI                  | tpi                     | | VRM                  | vrm                     | | Profile Curvature    | pcurv                   | | Tangential Curvature | tcurv                   | | Eastness             | eastness                | | Northness            | northness               |

title: Explore available variables and parameters — var_explore
text: Prints helpful information

title: Explore available variables and parameters — var_explore
code: # Show available sources var_explore() #>  #> ── Available data sources: ── #>  #>  #>  #> ── Climate Data:  #> • worldclim: WorldClim 2.1 climate data #> • chelsa: CHELSA climatologies v2.1 #> • chelsa_cmip5: CHELSA CMIP5 future projections #> • chelsa_bioclimplus: Extended bioclimatic variables #> • climate_stability: Climate stability indices #>  #> ── Land Cover:  #> • esa_landcover: ESA Land Cover CCI #> • consensus_landcover: Consensus land cover (Tuanmu & Jetz 2014) #>  #> ── Terrain & Cloud:  #> • topography: Elevation, slope, aspect, roughness, TRI, TPI #> • cloud: Cloud cover frequency from MODIS #>  #> ── Vegetation:  #> • ndvi: NDVI time series from MODIS/GIMMS #> • heterogeneity: Spectral heterogeneity metrics #>  #> ── Water & Drought:  #> • spectre: SPEI, SPI, PDSI drought indices #> • freshwater: HydroSHEDS hydrological variables #> • aridity: Global Aridity Index and PET #>  #> ── Other Environmental:  #> • hwsd: Harmonized World Soil Database #> • wind: Global Wind Atlas  # Show variables for WorldClim var_explore(source = "worldclim", what = "variables") #>  #> ── Available variables for "worldclim": ── #>  #> • bioclim: 19 bioclimatic variables (bio1-bio19) #> • tmean: Monthly mean temperature #> • tmin: Monthly minimum temperature #> • tmax: Monthly maximum temperature #> • prec: Monthly precipitation #> • srad: Solar radiation #> • wind: Wind speed #> • vapr: Water vapor pressure

what: Initialize the Environmental Variable Retrieval Pipeline — var_get
title: Initialize the Environmental Variable Retrieval Pipeline — var_get
text: `var_get()` entry point **envar** package workflow. defines spatial extent, resolution, coordinate reference system (CRS) study area.

title: Initialize the Environmental Variable Retrieval Pipeline — var_get
code: var_get(   shape = NULL,   country = NULL,   continent = NULL,   buffer = 0,   res = NULL,   path = NULL,   crs = NULL )

title: Initialize the Environmental Variable Retrieval Pipeline — var_get
text: shape `sf` object representing study area. can : * **Polygons:** defining region interest. * **Points:** defining specific sampling locations. `shape` provided, `country` `continent` ignored. `shape` provided, must already WGS84 projection. country Character. English name country (e.g., `"Italy"`, `"Viet Nam"`). Used generate extent `shape` NULL. continent Character. English name continent (e.g., `"Europe"`, `"Africa"`). Used generate extent `shape` `country` NULL. buffer Numeric. buffer distance **kilometers** expand extent. * **polygons/countries**: area expanded outwards distance. * **points**: circular buffer radius drawn around point, effectively   converting study area polygons (useful downloading background raster data   around occurrence points). * Default `0`. res Numeric. target spatial resolution **kilometers** (approximate). * controls cell size output raster stack. * Must positive integer (e.g., `1`, `5`, `10`). * Default `1` (approx. 30 arc-seconds). * *Note:* Downstream functions resample downloaded data match resolution. path Character. Optional path local directory saving intermediate files outputs. (Currently reserved future implementation). crs Character Numeric. Coordinate Reference System **final output**. * Can EPSG code (e.g., `4326`, `3035`), PROJ4 string, WKT. * `NULL` (default), pipeline uses standard WGS84 (EPSG:4326). * specified, downstream environmental layers projected CRS   processing.

title: Initialize the Environmental Variable Retrieval Pipeline — var_get
text: `list` object (class `envar_par`) containing:   * `grid`: template `SpatRaster` defining resolution extent.   * `mask`: `sf` object defining exact study area boundaries.   * `res`: resolution used.   * `bbox`: bounding box study area.   * `crs`: target coordinate reference system.   * `type`: type input ("polygon" "point").

title: Initialize the Environmental Variable Retrieval Pipeline — var_get
text: function download data . Instead, creates standardized spatial template (grid) processes point locations passed downstream functions (like `chelsa()`, `worldclim()`, `topography()`, etc.) ensure retrieved variables perfectly aligned stacked. ## works 1. **Extent Definition:** can define study area using shapefile (`sf` object),    `country` name, `continent` name. 2. **Resolution:** `res` argument sets target resolution kilometers (approximate    equator, based arc-seconds). 3. **Buffering:** optional buffer can applied expand study area create    sampling radius around points. 4. **Output:**    - input **polygon** (country/continent), returns list containing      target `SpatRaster` grid vector mask.    - input **points** (without buffer), returns point coordinates extraction.    - input **points buffer**, creates polygon geometry around points      returns grid, allowing download raster data area surrounding points.

title: Initialize the Environmental Variable Retrieval Pipeline — var_get
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(envar)  # ---------------------------------------------------------------- # Example 1: Working with a Country (Polygons) # ---------------------------------------------------------------- # Initialize a workflow for Italy at 1km resolution # and pipe it into a download function data_stack <- var_get(country = "Italy", res = 1) %>%   topography(vars = "elevation", topo_source = "GMTED")  # ---------------------------------------------------------------- # Example 2: Working with Points (Extraction) # ---------------------------------------------------------------- # Define some points (e.g., species occurrences) my_points <- data.frame(lon = c(12.5, 13.0), lat = c(42.0, 42.5)) my_points_sf <- sf::st_as_sf(my_points, coords = c("lon", "lat"), crs = 4326)  # Initialize extraction workflow (res is ignored for pure point extraction) point_data <- var_get(shape = my_points_sf) %>%   worldclim(vars = "bio")  # ---------------------------------------------------------------- # Example 3: Working with Points + Buffer (Background Raster) # ---------------------------------------------------------------- # Create a 50km buffer around points to get environmental rasters # for the surrounding area (e.g., for pseudo-absence generation) background_stack <- var_get(shape = my_points_sf, buffer = 50, res = 5) %>%   chelsa(vars = "prec")  # ---------------------------------------------------------------- # Example 4: Reprojection # ---------------------------------------------------------------- # Get data for France, but project everything to Lambert-93 (EPSG:2154) france_data <- var_get(country = "France", res = 1, crs = 2154) %>%   accessibility(vars = "cities1") } # }

what: Download WorldClim Climate Data (Historical & Future) — worldclim
title: Download WorldClim Climate Data (Historical & Future) — worldclim
text: function builds URLs downloads WorldClim climate data based variables, years, climate models, scenarios. supports Historical (v2.1, 1970-2000) Future (CMIP6) data.

title: Download WorldClim Climate Data (Historical & Future) — worldclim
code: worldclim(   x,   vars,   years = NULL,   months = NULL,   gcm = NULL,   rcp = NULL,   ssp = NULL,   ... )

title: Download WorldClim Climate Data (Historical & Future) — worldclim
text: x SpatRaster sf object define area interest. vars character vector variables download. Accepts following names (case-insensitive): Temperature: "tmin", "tmax", "tavg" (average temp) Precipitation: "prec", "pr" Bioclimatic: "bio" (19), specific e.g., "bio1", "bio12" Physical: "srad" (solar radiation), "wind" (wind speed),         "vapr" (vapor pressure), "elev" (elevation) years character vector years periods. **Historical**: Use "1970-2000" ("historical"). **Future**: Use "2021-2040", "2041-2060", "2061-2080", "2081-2100". months numeric vector (1–12) specifying months download. applies monthly historical variables (tmin, tmax, tavg, prec, srad, wind, vapr). Ignored bioclimatic variables, elevation, future data. gcm character vector General Circulation Models (Future data). ssp character numeric vector Shared Socioeconomic Pathways (e.g., "126", "585"). ... Additional arguments (currently unused).

title: Download WorldClim Climate Data (Historical & Future) — worldclim
text: SpatRaster stack data.frame extracted values.

title: Download WorldClim Climate Data (Historical & Future) — worldclim
text: Uses helper function `download_file` handle downloads. raster layer downloaded (unzipped necessary), processed (cropped/masked/resampled), added output stack immediately, temporary files deleted minimize memory use.

title: Download WorldClim Climate Data (Historical & Future) — worldclim
text: Fick, Stephen E., Robert J. Hijmans. "WorldClim 2: new 1‐km spatial resolution climate surfaces global land areas." International journal climatology 37, . 12 (2017): 4302-4315. DOI: https://doi.org/10.1002/joc.5086
